outlook.headline: 6G demand is starting to broaden, but still early
outlook.summary: The read-through for the next 1-2 quarters is constructive: multiple suppliers are seeing early 6G-related pull from RF transition plans, component requests, new product activity, and ecosystem coalition-building. The upside is most obvious for Skyworks, Vishay, Qualcomm, and Marvell, while the main watch-out is that current demand is still embryonic and mentions remain sparse despite the recent uptick.

drivers.label: RF transition catalyst
drivers.detail: An upcoming major RF transition is starting to pull demand forward.

drivers.label: Early component requests
drivers.detail: Customer requests for 6G parts suggest demand is moving from concept to initial sourcing.

drivers.label: Standards and new product cycle
drivers.detail: New 6G-related products and standards imply future content growth and a wider addressable opportunity.

drivers.label: Ecosystem alignment
drivers.detail: Positive partner feedback and coalition-building point to stronger industry support for 6G.

drivers.label: Incremental workload demand
drivers.detail: Running 5G and 6G workloads with AI on the same hardware adds incremental telecom silicon demand.

risks: 6G remains early-stage, so requests may not convert into near-term volume
Standards or ecosystem coordination could slip, delaying content growth
Mention intensity is still low despite the latest uptick, limiting visibility into adoption pace
